# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: External PR Testing

on:
  pull_request_target:
    branches:
      - main

jobs:
  build-and-test:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v2

      - name: Set up Python
        uses: actions/setup-python@v2
        with:
          python-version: '3.8'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: |
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r requirements.txt

      - name: Run tests
        run: pytest

      - name: Post results as comment
        uses: actions/github-script@v2
        with:
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          script: |
            const pr = context.payload.pull_request;
            const { data: comments } = await octokit.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: pr.number
            });
            const commentExists = comments.data.some(comment => comment.body.includes('Test Results'));
            if (!commentExists) {
              await octokit.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: pr.number,
                body: 'Test Results:\n- Test 1 Passed\n- Test 2 Failed'
              });
            }
